intent: We are not building the Forge Agent architecture. We are building a Mac Consensus Dev Agent called Crafted that will use consensus AI to develop high quality software. Use all the documents in the local repo to determine the architecture.
scope_statement:
Build the Crafted Dev Agent — a native macOS two-process application consisting of a Swift/SwiftUI shell (UI, Touch ID authentication, Keychain credential storage, XPC communication, process management) and a Python 3.12 backend (consensus AI code generation via Claude + GPT-4o, build pipeline orchestration, GitHub integration, document store with FAISS embeddings, multi-agent coordination, holistic code review, TRD development workflow, cross-run learning via build memory and build rules). The system transforms plain-language build intents into tested, CI-passing GitHub pull requests through a confidence-gated scope phase, PRD decomposition, PR planning with type routing, parallel dual-model code generation with Claude arbitration, self-correction, lint gating, iterative fix loops, and operator-gated approval — all specified across 16 TRDs in forge-docs/.

Clarifications:
  - review the TRDs and if the data is missing ensure they are included in the PRDs.
  - review the TRDs and if the data is missing ensure they are included in the PRDs. Ensure we capture that it needs fixing
  - review the TRDs and if the data is missing ensure they are included in the PRDs. Choose the best practices
  - review the TRDs and if the data is missing ensure they are included in the PRDs. Choose the best practices

Operator on 'What is the build prioritization order across the 16 TRDs? S':
Select the best build process and order the PRDs so that the code is built the best way from start to finish

Operator on 'TRD-8 v2.0 introduces Figma pipeline UI and sketch interpret':
should be included

Operator on 'The documents reference both 'forge-agent' branch naming (ke':
crafted in all new identifiers, module names and file paths

Operator on 'Should the build target the full CONSULTANT mode (TRD-7 §21)':
build target the full CONSULTANT mode (TRD-7 §21) and FOUNDER mode (TRD-7 §3-6) in the initial scope

prd_plan.title: Foundation Architecture, Naming Migration, and Repository Skeleton
Security Baseline and Trust Boundary Enforcement
Swift Shell Core: App Lifecycle, Auth, Keychain, and Process Management
Cross-Process Transport: XPC/Unix Socket Channel and Handshake Protocol
Backend Runtime Startup, Health, Version Handshake, and Graceful Shutdown
Document Store, FAISS Retrieval, Injection Defense, and Build Rules Loading
Consensus Engine and Language-Aware Multi-Model Code Generation
Core Build Pipeline: Confidence Gate, PRD Decomposition, PR Planning, and Operator-Gated Execution
Pre-Test Quality Engine: Repo Context, Self-Correction, Lint Gate, and Fix Loop
Recovery, Thread State, Checkpoints, Build Memory, and Build Rules Synthesis
GitHub Integration, Branch/PR Lifecycle, CI Polling, and Repository Bootstrap
Multi-Agent Coordination, Build Ledger, Journals, and Team Knowledge Sharing
TRD Development Workflow with FOUNDER and CONSULTANT Modes
Holistic Code Review Workflow and Review Fix PRs
SwiftUI Product Experience: Build Stream, Navigator, Context Panel, Gates, and Operator Modes
Figma Pipeline, Sketch Interpretation, and Multi-Modal Design-to-Code Input
CI Infrastructure, macOS Runner, Workflow Hardening, and Release Validation
Comprehensive Testing, Regression Contracts, and Release Readiness Validation

1.title: Repository skeleton: top-level directory structure and placeholder files
1.summary: Creates the canonical directory tree for the entire Crafted Dev Agent repository. Includes empty placeholder files (.gitkeep or minimal __init__.py) for all directories: Swift targets (CraftedAppShell, CraftedAuthKit, CraftedKeychainKit, CraftedXPCBridge, CraftedProcessManager, CraftedBuildStream, CraftedSettings, CraftedDocImport), Python src/ modules, tests/, forge-standards/, crafted-docs/, .github/workflows/, scripts/, schemas/, tools/, and resources/. No logic — purely structural scaffolding so all subsequent PRs have stable paths to land in.

2.title: Canonical naming conventions and identifier registry document
2.summary: Creates forge-standards/CONVENTIONS.md — the single authoritative naming registry document per FR-1.1. Enumerates every identifier namespace (Swift modules, bundle IDs, os_log subsystems, Keychain service names, XPC socket paths, environment variables, GitHub branch prefixes, CI workflow filenames, coordination directories, application display name, Python bootstrap signals) with canonical Crafted prefix, legacy prefix retention decisions, and rationale. Documents the sole legacy exception: `forge-agent/build/` branch prefix retained per TRD-5 §6.2 and AGENTS.md. Includes naming collision resolution rules and best-practice decisions for any TRD gaps.

3.title: TRD build prioritization order and gap analysis document
3.summary: Creates forge-standards/BUILD_ORDER.md documenting the canonical build prioritization order across all 16 TRDs, resolving inter-TRD dependencies, documenting any TRD gaps found (missing data, naming collisions, path placement ambiguities), and recording best-practice decisions to fix those gaps. Covers FOUNDER mode (TRD-7 §3-6) and CONSULTANT mode (TRD-7 §21) scoping, Figma pipeline inclusion (TRD-8 v2.0), and cross-TRD dependency graph. This is the authoritative sequencing reference for all subsequent PRDs.

4.title: Repository and module map document
4.summary: Creates forge-standards/MODULE_MAP.md — the single authoritative repository/module map. Covers all Swift targets (CraftedAppShell, CraftedAuthKit, CraftedKeychainKit, CraftedXPCBridge, CraftedProcessManager, CraftedBuildStream, CraftedSettings, CraftedDocImport), Python src/ modules (config, models, contracts, xpc, workflow, agents, consensus, review, github_integration, document_store, build_memory, figma, runtime), workflow files, test directories, standards docs, crafted-docs/, scripts, schemas, and generated documents. Documents cross-process ownership boundaries (Swift shell vs Python backend) and which TRD each module implements.

5.title: Version constants and semantic version ownership
5.summary: Implements the canonical version constants for both Swift and Python sides. Swift: a CraftedVersion.swift file in CraftedAppShell with static version string, build number, and semantic version components. Python: src/config/version.py with VERSION, BUILD, SEMVER tuple, and version_string() utility. Both files are the single source of truth for their respective processes, with a shared VERSION file at repo root that both read or are generated from. Includes a scripts/bump_version.py helper for synchronized version bumps. Also creates forge-standards/VERSIONING.md documenting the versioning strategy.

6.title: Feature flags system with TOML config and environment overrides
6.summary: Implements the feature flag system for both Swift and Python. Python: src/config/feature_flags.py with a FeatureFlags dataclass loaded from a crafted-config.toml file and overridable via environment variables (CRAFTED_FF_* prefix). Covers flags for: consensus_enabled, figma_pipeline_enabled, consultant_mode_enabled, founder_mode_enabled, build_memory_enabled, holistic_review_enabled, self_correction_max_retries, lint_gating_strict, and operator_approval_required. Swift: CraftedSettings/FeatureFlags.swift reading from the same TOML config or receiving flags via XPC handshake. Includes the default crafted-config.toml template at repo root and forge-standards/FEATURE_FLAGS.md documenting all flags.

7.title: Startup environment variables and configuration loader
7.summary: Implements the startup environment variable contract and unified configuration loader. Python: src/config/env.py defining all CRAFTED_* environment variables (CRAFTED_XPC_SOCKET, CRAFTED_XPC_NONCE, CRAFTED_WORKSPACE, CRAFTED_LOG_DIR, CRAFTED_GITHUB_TOKEN, CRAFTED_CLAUDE_API_KEY, CRAFTED_OPENAI_API_KEY, CRAFTED_CONFIG_PATH) with validation, defaults, and error messages. src/config/loader.py that merges environment variables → config file → defaults into a single CraftedConfig dataclass. src/runtime/errors.py for typed configuration errors. Swift: CraftedProcessManager/EnvironmentBuilder.swift that constructs the environment dictionary passed to the Python subprocess. Per TRD-12 §3.2.

8.title: Shared JSON envelope and XPC message schema definitions
8.summary: Defines the cross-language JSON message envelope and all XPC message schemas used for Swift↔Python communication. Python side: src/contracts/messages.py with Pydantic v2 models for the envelope (id, type, timestamp, payload, error), and typed message classes for all known message types (handshake, auth_request, auth_response, build_command, build_status, log_event, etc.). src/contracts/handshake.py for handshake-specific models. Swift side: CraftedXPCBridge/Models/XPCMessage.swift and CraftedXPCBridge/Models/HandshakeMessage.swift with Codable structs mirroring the Python schemas. Includes JSON Schema files at schemas/. Also creates forge-standards/SCHEMAS.md documenting the schema contract. Per TRD-1 §6 and TRD-12 §3.

9.title: Tests for naming conventions, module map integrity, and version sync
9.summary: Validation tests that programmatically verify: (1) All directories listed in MODULE_MAP.md exist in the repo. (2) All identifiers in CONVENTIONS.md use the Crafted prefix (except the documented legacy exception). (3) No Python source file in src/ contains stale 'forge' string literals (excluding the branch naming pattern). (4) The VERSION file, Python version.py, and Swift CraftedVersion.swift all agree on the version string. (5) All CRAFTED_* env vars documented in env.py are listed in CONVENTIONS.md. Acts as the migration checklist validator per FR-1.2.

10.title: Swift Package.swift and Xcode project scaffolding
10.summary: Creates the Swift Package.swift that defines all 8 Swift targets as libraries/executables with correct inter-target dependencies: CraftedAppShell (depends on all others), CraftedAuthKit (standalone), CraftedKeychainKit (standalone), CraftedXPCBridge (depends on Models), CraftedProcessManager (depends on CraftedXPCBridge), CraftedBuildStream (depends on CraftedXPCBridge), CraftedSettings (standalone), CraftedDocImport (standalone). Includes test targets for each. Sets minimum deployment target to macOS 14.0, Swift 5.9+. Bundle identifier uses ai.yousource.crafted per CONVENTIONS.md. Creates Sources/ subdirectories with minimal stub files for compilation.

11.title: Python project configuration: pyproject.toml, requirements, and tooling
11.summary: Creates the Python project configuration: pyproject.toml with project metadata (name=crafted-dev-agent), Python 3.12 requirement, all dependencies (pydantic, faiss-cpu, openai, anthropic, httpx, GitPython, toml, rich, etc.), dev dependencies (pytest, ruff, mypy, pytest-asyncio), and tool configurations for ruff (linting rules), mypy (strict mode), and pytest (test paths). Includes a requirements.txt for pip fallback, a .python-version file (3.12), and scripts/setup_dev.sh for one-command dev environment setup. Configures src/ as the package root.

12.title: Tests for XPC message schemas and cross-language contract validation
12.summary: Test suite validating the XPC message contract: (1) test_xpc_schema_validation.py verifying all Python Pydantic models validate against the JSON Schema files. (2) test_xpc_envelope_roundtrip.py verifying serialization/deserialization roundtrip for every message type. (3) test_xpc_schema_completeness.py verifying every message type in the JSON Schema has a corresponding Python model and that all required fields are present. (4) test_xpc_security.py verifying nonce field is required, timestamp is ISO-8601, and payload size limits are enforced. (5) test_handshake_examples.py and test_backend_event_examples.py with fixture-based cross-language compatibility checks. These tests are the cross-language contract safety net.

13.title: Tests for feature flags, config loader, and environment variables
13.summary: Comprehensive test suite for the configuration subsystem: (1) test_feature_flags_defaults.py verifying all flags have correct defaults from crafted-config.toml. (2) test_feature_flags_env_override.py verifying CRAFTED_FF_* environment variables correctly override config file values. (3) test_config_loader_merge.py verifying the env → config → defaults merge precedence. (4) test_env_validation.py verifying that missing required env vars produce clear error messages. (5) test_config_loader_integration.py end-to-end test loading a complete config. (6) test_startup_env_contract.py validating the startup environment invariants. Tests cover both FOUNDER and CONSULTANT mode flag combinations.

14.title: Cross-process ownership boundary documentation and XPC protocol contract
14.summary: Creates forge-standards/OWNERSHIP_BOUNDARIES.md documenting the precise responsibility split between Swift shell and Python backend. Swift shell owns: UI rendering, Touch ID/biometric auth, Keychain CRUD, process lifecycle management, XPC socket creation, user-facing settings, document import UI, operator approval gates. Python backend owns: AI model calls, consensus generation, build pipeline orchestration, GitHub API interactions, FAISS document store, build memory/rules, PR planning, code generation, lint gating, self-correction loops. Also creates forge-standards/XPC_PROTOCOL.md detailing the full message flow: handshake → auth → command → streaming status → completion, with sequence diagrams in Mermaid.

15.title: Legacy forge string audit script and naming migration checklist
15.summary: Implements tools/audit_legacy_forge_refs.py — an automated script per FR-1.2 that scans all Python source files in src/ and Swift files for string literals containing the old 'forge' prefix (excluding the documented branch naming exception pattern `forge-agent/build/` and forge-standards/ directory references). Outputs a structured JSON report with file path, line number, matched string, and suggested replacement. Also produces crafted-docs/MIGRATION_CHECKLIST.md and forge-standards/NAMING_MIGRATION_CHECKLIST.md. This script is designed to be run in CI and locally as a pre-commit check to prevent naming regression.

16.title: Swift runtime environment and launch configuration models
16.summary: Adds the Swift-side runtime configuration models for backend launch settings, environment propagation, socket paths, log directories, and feature flag toggles aligned to the shared standards. Creates CraftedAppShell/Configuration/BackendLaunchConfiguration.swift and CraftedAppShell/Configuration/RuntimeEnvironment.swift with typed models that mirror the Python-side environment contract. Extends CraftedProcessManager/EnvironmentBuilder.swift with the full environment dictionary construction logic.

17.title: CI workflow files for Python linting, testing, and Swift build
17.summary: Creates .github/workflows/crafted-ci.yml (Python CI: lint with ruff, type-check with mypy, run pytest on ubuntu-latest with Python 3.12) and .github/workflows/crafted-ci-macos.yml (Swift CI: build all Swift targets on macos-14 runner, run Swift tests). Both workflows trigger on push/PR to main and forge-agent/build/** branches. Python CI includes a naming convention check step that runs the audit script and standards tests. Workflow filenames follow CONVENTIONS.md canonical names. Includes reusable setup actions for Python and Swift environments.

18.title: TRD gap analysis results, best-practice decisions, and PRD-to-TRD traceability
18.summary: Creates crafted-docs/TRD_GAP_ANALYSIS.md documenting all gaps, ambiguities, and conflicts discovered across the 16 TRDs during PRD-001 implementation. For each gap: TRD reference, description of the issue, best-practice decision made, and rationale. Covers: naming collision resolution between TRD-1 and TRD-12 on socket paths, TRD-8 v2.0 Figma pipeline integration points, FOUNDER vs CONSULTANT mode feature flag mapping to TRD-7 sections, missing error handling specifications, and any TRD cross-references that were found to be inconsistent. Also creates forge-standards/TRD_TO_PRD_TRACEABILITY.md mapping each TRD to the PRD(s) that implement it and forge-standards/PRD_BUILD_SEQUENCE.md documenting the recommended PRD execution order across all 16 TRDs.
